summary: Can a simple SIP actually build real wealth, or is that just a story we keep repeating? Part 1 with Roopa Venkatkrishnan. In this episode of This or That with Bhartendra, Roopa Venkatkrishnan breaks down what actually happens when you stay invested for decades, why market corrections are a feature and not a flaw, and why the negativity around mutual funds right now misses the bigger picture.

chapters.label: Can you really make money with SIPs?
₹10,000 a month to ₹18–22 crore: the real numbers
Why an SIP is built for retirement
Gold, real estate, and the SIP nobody noticed
Half of all SIPs are getting cancelled
Why volatility is a feature, not a bug
What an SIP actually is: rupee cost averaging
FII selling and the India growth story
The only asset class that beats inflation
"SIPs don't make money": the diabetes answer

tool.reason: This episode is really about your mix. Put in your numbers and see the equity, debt, gold and international split that actually fits you.
